summary: Matched Zelda's Din's Fire item object and finished the Din's Fire explosion item object. The PR converted both itzeldadinfire.c and itzeldadinfireexplode.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, named the Din's Fire state table symbol as ItemStateTable_ZeldaDinFire, added a typed xDD4 item variable struct for Din's Fire, and replaced many stubbed/UNK prototypes with concrete item, collision, spawn, animation, physics, reflection, and destroy logic.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed Din's Fire functions in src/melee/it/items/itzeldadinfire.c, including it_802C3AFC, it_802C3BAC, it_2725_Logic65_Destroyed, it_802C3D44, it_802C3D74, motion animation/physics/collision handlers, and reflected/clanked/absorbed handlers.
Added ItemStateTable_ZeldaDinFire[] with two item states and updated it_2725.c to reference it instead of the raw symbol it_803F76F8.
Renamed the GALE01 symbol it_803F76F8 to ItemStateTable_ZeldaDinFire in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.

Finished itzeldadinfireexplode.c by adding its ItemStateTable it_803F7740 and making small matching-sensitive source changes, including spawn position assignment order, variable declaration order, and using ip->xDC8_word.flags.x13 instead of ip->xDCC_flag.b0.
Added itZeldaDinFire_ItemVars to itCommonItems.h with a static size assertion of 36 bytes and inserted it into Item.xDD4_itemVar in src/melee/it/types.h.
Updated itzeldadinfire.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and anonymous parameters to typed signatures using Item_GObj*, CollData*, Vec3*, bool, and void return types.
Marked both melee/it/items/itzeldadinfire.c and melee/it/items/itzeldadinfireexplode.c as Matching in configure.py.
Adjusted GALE01 split boundaries for .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 around itzeldadinfire.c and itzeldadinfireexplode.c.
smart_moves: Promoted the raw state table symbol to the descriptive name ItemStateTable_ZeldaDinFire and wired that name through the item logic table, making the item-state relationship searchable and less address-dependent.
Introduced a dedicated xDD4_itemVar union member for Din's Fire rather than continuing to access item variables through anonymous padding or unknown fields.
Used STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(itZeldaDinFire_ItemVars) == 36) to protect the inferred item variable layout.
Kept the local special-attribute struct in itzeldadinfire.c as an offset-based float layout, which is evidence-grounded when semantic names for the attributes are not yet known.
Captured matching-sensitive details in the explosion object, such as assigning spawn.prev_pos before spawn.pos and preserving variable declaration order.
Factored repeated state-transition behavior into a static inline helper itZeldadinfire_UnkMotion0_Anim_inline, likely helping match repeated codegen while keeping the source readable.
decomp_lessons: When an item file becomes matching, update configure.py, symbols.txt, splits.txt, headers, and the central item logic table together; matching status is not just a .c-file change.
For item-specific xDD4 data, add a named struct in itCommonItems.h and a named union member in Item.xDD4_itemVar once enough layout is known.
Prefer concrete prototypes in headers as soon as function behavior is known; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with Item_GObj*, CollData*, Vec3*, bool, and void improves future callsite decompilation.
State-table symbols should be named by item and purpose when identified, e.g. ItemStateTable_ZeldaDinFire, and then referenced from ItemLogicTable instead of raw it_803F-address names.
Matching item spawn code can depend on exact assignment order for SpawnItem fields, even when the final values are equivalent.
Bitfield aliases matter for matching and correctness; this PR used ip->xDC8_word.flags.x13 in places related to item flag clearing rather than an older xDCC_flag.b0 access.
Collision checks can often be expressed from CollData env_flags with direction-dependent masks, as in it_802C3AFC checking vertical and horizontal velocity signs before selecting env flag masks.
naming_conventions: Use ItemStateTable_<CharacterOrItemName> for identified item state tables; this PR uses ItemStateTable_ZeldaDinFire.
Use it<itemname>_ItemVars for item-specific xDD4 variable structs; this PR adds itZeldaDinFire_ItemVars.
Motion handlers retain the existing pattern itZeldadinfire_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll when state semantics are not yet named.
Logic callbacks retain it_2725_Logic65_* names for item logic table callbacks such as Destroyed, Reflected, Clanked, and Absorbed.
Function parameters were renamed from generic arg0 to context-specific names like gobj, cd, vec, facing_dir, parent_gobj, and scale where supported by the implementation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved itzeldadinfire.c and itzeldadinfireexplode.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...) after source changes.
Adjusted .data/.sdata/.sdata2 split endpoints to account for newly matched object data sizes around the Din's Fire files.
Used PAD_STACK in functions where stack layout required padding, including itZeldadinfire_UnkMotion0_Coll and the explosion spawn function.
Preserved exact floating-point math patterns with cosf, sinf, M_PI, and M_PI_2 for Din's Fire trajectory and reflection behavior.
Used an inline helper for a repeated animation/state transition sequence that destroys existing effects, changes item animation state, spawns a new effect, sets xDF4, and initializes life timer.
Maintained declaration and assignment ordering in itzeldadinfireexplode.c, suggesting small source-order changes were needed for matching.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only available human-text signal is the PR title; no PR body excerpt or reviewer rationale was provided.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itZeldadinfire_UnkMotion0/1 handlers to semantic state names.
Search for later refinements to itZeldaDinFire_ItemVars field names xDD8, xDDC, xDE0, xDE4, xDE8, xDEC, xDF0, and xDF4.
Check whether it_803F7740 for itzeldadinfireexplode is later renamed to a descriptive ItemStateTable_* symbol.
Compare other projectile item PRs that use xDC8_word.flags.x13 instead of xDCC_flag.b0.
Search for Din's Fire attribute struct naming or field naming work after PR 1926.
